summary: AFI's documentation surface carries substantial drift against the portable-protocol north star. (1) Stale/nonexistent repo names persist in current docs: afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md still documents afi-pipeline and afi-agents as core repos with interdependency diagrams, the org map and afi-protocol onboarding still list the archived afi-agents/afi-construct, and several metadata files (afi-math/.afi-codex.json) name repos (afi-engine, afi-token-finalized) that do not exist in the workspace (28 afi-* repos + .github + afi-xerc20; no afi-pipeline/afi-agents/afi-construct present). (2) The contradiction register can be expanded along two axes documented here: reference-impl-presented-as-law (Orchestrator Doctrine 'Authoritative', reactor 'is the orchestrator', 'DAG is law') and Mongo-tunnel-vision (AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT equates the TSSD vault with MongoDB), plus an internal doc-hierarchy conflict in which README/AGENTS/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS/Doctrine each self-declare authority while none names AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as the top normative reference. (3) Doc classification: only specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md and the investigation charter are normative direction-setters; the architecture/status/atlas/pipeline-audit/doctrine files are reference-implementation descriptions. The afi-token stale-metadata item (.droid.json hardhat/sepolia + .afi-codex.json src/AFICoordinator.sol while the repo is Foundry/Base with src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol) is captured here as a P3 stale-arch-docs entry per Theme C handoff.

answers.question: J1: Locate stale / renamed / nonexistent repo-name references in docs (e.g. historical afi-pipeline, afi-agents, afi-construct) and cite them.
answers.answer: Stale repo names are still embedded in current (non-archived) docs even though those repos do not exist in the workspace. GROUND TRUTH: the local org tree contains 28 afi-* repos plus .github and afi-xerc20, and NONE of afi-pipeline, afi-agents, or afi-construct is present; the in-repo census reflects this (afi-docs/AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md:5-29 lists every present repo and contains no afi-pipeline/afi-agents/afi-construct row). The north star explicitly flags these as stale: 'Also check for renamed, archived, or external-reference repos (e.g. historical afi-pipeline, afi-agents references in older docs)' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:181). STALE REFERENCES STILL LIVE: (a) afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md documents 'afi-pipeline' as a core repo with a 'DAG execution engine' (afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:24) and 'afi-agents' as a collection of agent implementations (afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:54), wires them into the interdependency diagram 'afi-gateway -> afi-pipeline -> afi-reactor' and 'afi-factory -> afi-agents' (afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:168,170) and into the development workflow (afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:185-187,197-198); this whole map is stale. (b) The org-profile map lists afi-agents and afi-construct (correctly under an 'Archived (Historical Only)' heading, so this one is accurately classified) at .github/profile/README.md:73-74. (c) afi-protocol onboarding still hyperlinks the archived afi-agents as a live 'agent configs & runtime' repo (afi-protocol/contributor-manifest/onboarding.md:9). (d) Scattered afi-agents path references persist in the reactor doctrine copy (afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:20 'Pipeline logic scattered in afi-core or afi-agents'), in lore (afi-docs/lore/episode-2_return_of_the_mentor.md:15 'Registry-Path: afi-agents/registry/mentor_registry.ts') and in a spec snippet pointer (afi-docs/specs/mentor_protocol.md:115 'See afi-agents/examples/handleSignal.ts'). (e) Beyond afi-docs, codex metadata names nonexistent consumer repos: afi-math/.afi-codex.json:21-22 lists consumers 'afi-engine' and 'afi-token-finalized', neither of which exists locally. The afi-pipeline name appears only inside afi-docs (the historical Repository Map and the investigation/portable specs that flag it); it is not a live repo anywhere.
answers.evidence: afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:24; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:54; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:168; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:185-187; afi-docs/AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md:5-29; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:181; .github/profile/README.md:73-74; afi-protocol/contributor-manifest/onboarding.md:9; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:20; afi-docs/lore/episode-2_return_of_the_mentor.md:15; afi-docs/specs/mentor_protocol.md:115; afi-math/.afi-codex.json:21-22

answers.question: J2: How should the Phase-1 contradiction register be expanded with documentation/drift conflicts (which doc-vs-code tensions and severities does the docs surface add)?
answers.answer: The docs surface contributes documentation-drift contradictions in three buckets that expand the draft register (drafts/AFI_CONTRADICTION_REGISTER.draft.md rows 29-34, 64, 103). BUCKET 1 - reference-impl-presented-as-law: a full copy of the reactor Orchestrator Doctrine lives in the docs hub headed 'Status: Authoritative' (afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5), declares 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI' (:11) and 'The DAG is law / Every signal path ... must be expressible as a Reactor DAG' (:39), and closes 'This doctrine is authoritative. All afi-reactor code must comply.' (:290) - directly against the north star's reframe 'reactor is reference orchestrator; protocol law is conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133); classify P2 reactor-only (the in-reactor README/AGENTS originals are P1, see Theme E). BUCKET 2 - Mongo-tunnel-vision in docs: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md presents the TSSD vault as MongoDB ('MongoDB TSSD vault persistence (Phase 1 complete)' afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:16; 'afi-infra - TSSD vault (MongoDB), types, schemas' :41) with no 'reference engine' caveat, while vault.schema.json allows four engines (Theme B/D, Mongo-only); classify P2 Mongo-only. BUCKET 3 - internal doc-hierarchy / authority conflict: README, AGENTS, ARCHITECTURE_STATUS and the Doctrine each assert authority over the others (afi-docs/AGENTS.md:4 'If AGENTS.md conflicts with README or docs, AGENTS.md wins.'; afi-docs/AGENTS.md:25 'Codex + AOS are truth sources. Whitepaper is narrative, not canonical.'; afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4 'Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today.'; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:290 'This doctrine is authoritative.') yet none names AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as the top normative doc, so the north star is hosted but not asserted as canonical; classify P2 other. Additional pure stale-arch-docs rows to add: the afi-token metadata mismatch (see J3 / finding #1), afi-math/.afi-codex.json stale consumers (afi-math/.afi-codex.json:21-22), the broken SUMMARY.md sitemap (afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:13-18), and the AFI_Repository_Map stale-name row (J1). All draft rows in these buckets currently read 'Verified? = no'; Phase 3 should re-open each cited line (the citations here are re-opened and current).
answers.evidence: afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:11; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:39; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:290; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133; afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:16; afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:41; afi-docs/AGENTS.md:4; afi-docs/AGENTS.md:25; afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4; afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:13-18; afi-math/.afi-codex.json:21-22

answers.question: J3: Classify the docs as normative vs reference vs archive, with rationale.
answers.answer: NORMATIVE (direction-setting protocol law for the docs hub): afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md - the portable-protocol charter that 'distinguish[es] normative rules from reference implementations' (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:7) - and the investigation charter specs/AFI_PROTOCOL_INVESTIGATION_PROMPT.md (methodology). These are documents, not enforced artifacts; the binding machine-checkable normative law lives outside afi-docs in afi-config schemas, afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts, afi-reactor VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC and afi-token/src/*.sol (Theme A). REFERENCE (accurate-but-implementation-specific descriptions that must be labeled 'reference', not law): afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md (calls itself 'Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today' and correctly notes a custom TS DAG, afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4,10), afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md (a duplicated reactor doctrine, 'Authoritative'), afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md, AFI_System_Atlas.md and AFI_Full_Architecture.md - all describe the reference spine (reactor + Mongo vault + BASE) and should carry a reference banner per the north star (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133). SHOULD-BE-ARCHIVED-OR-CORRECTED (stale, currently presented as live): afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md (stale afi-pipeline/afi-agents topology, J1) and afi-docs/SUMMARY.md (sitemap pointing at nonexistent files, afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:8-10,13-18). The repo's own metadata classifies it as active DOCS (afi-docs/.afi-codex.json) and README frames it as 'the official documentation hub' (afi-docs/README.md:6); the classification problem is intra-repo (mixing normative direction, reference description, and un-banner'd stale docs), not the repo type.
answers.evidence: afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:7; afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4; afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:10; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:24; afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:8-10; afi-docs/README.md:6; afi-docs/.afi-codex.json:4

findings.title: AFI_Repository_Map.md documents stale/nonexistent repos (afi-pipeline, afi-agents) as core repos with topology + workflow
findings.evidence: afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:24 '### afi-pipeline ... Signal processing pipeline with DAG execution'; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:54 '### afi-agents'; afi-docs/AFI_Repository_Map.md:168 'afi-gateway -> afi-pipeline -> afi-reactor'; neither repo exists locally and afi-docs/AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md:5-29 lists neither
findings.recommendation: Archive or rewrite AFI_Repository_Map.md against the current repo set; reconcile with AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md and the north-star repo inventory. Mark it superseded with an explicit supersession banner.

findings.title: afi-token metadata is stale / wrong-toolchain: .droid.json declares hardhat/sepolia and .afi-codex.json references non-existent src/AFICoordinator.sol while the repo is Foundry/Base (src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol)
findings.evidence: afi-token/.droid.json:3-4 '"preferred_chain": "sepolia", "toolchain": "hardhat"'; afi-token/.afi-codex.json:8 '"src/AFICoordinator.sol"' (no such file) vs actual afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:1 and AGENTS.md Foundry/Base toolchain (forge build); grep AFICoordinator in afi-token/src = 0 hits (only AFIMintCoordinator.sol)
findings.recommendation: Update afi-token/.droid.json toolchain->foundry and preferred_chain->base, and fix .afi-codex.json entrypoints to src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol (and src/AFISignalReceipt.sol). Carries the Theme C cross-repo handoff into the docs-drift register (stale-arch-docs).

findings.title: Internal doc-hierarchy conflict: README/AGENTS/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS/Doctrine each self-declare authority; none names the portable-protocol spec as the top normative doc
findings.evidence: afi-docs/AGENTS.md:4 'If AGENTS.md conflicts with README or docs, AGENTS.md wins.'; afi-docs/AGENTS.md:25 'Codex + AOS are truth sources.'; afi-docs/ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4 'Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today.'; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:290 'This doctrine is authoritative.' - none point to specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as canonical
findings.recommendation: Establish an explicit doc hierarchy that names AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as the top normative direction; scope AGENTS.md authority to editing behavior, not protocol semantics; mark ARCHITECTURE_STATUS/Doctrine as reference.

findings.title: Reactor Orchestrator Doctrine ('Authoritative', reactor is THE orchestrator, 'DAG is law') duplicated into the docs hub
findings.evidence: afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5 'Status: Authoritative'; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:11 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'; afi-docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:39 'The DAG is law' vs afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133 'Reframe: reactor is reference orchestrator'
findings.recommendation: Re-banner the docs-hub copy as reference orchestration; defer to Theme E for the in-reactor originals. Protocol law is conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions, not the reactor codebase.

findings.title: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT equates the TSSD vault with MongoDB (Mongo tunnel vision) with no reference-engine caveat
findings.evidence: afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:16 '- MongoDB TSSD vault persistence (Phase 1 complete)'; afi-docs/AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:41 'afi-infra - TSSD vault (MongoDB), types, schemas' vs multi-engine vault.schema.json (Theme B/D)
findings.recommendation: Label Mongo as the default reference vault engine and cite vault.schema.json's PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB support; align with the AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP and AFI_CONTRADICTION_REGISTER (Mongo-only).

findings.title: afi-math/.afi-codex.json lists stale/nonexistent consumer repos (afi-engine, afi-token-finalized)
findings.evidence: afi-math/.afi-codex.json:21-22 'consumers: ["afi-engine", "afi-token-finalized", ...]' - neither repo exists locally (workspace has afi-token, not afi-token-finalized; no afi-engine)
findings.recommendation: Update consumers to the real repo names (afi-token, afi-reactor, afi-core, afi-mint, afi-config, afi-plugins); add a codex-metadata lint that resolves repo names against the org inventory.

findings.title: Broken SUMMARY.md sitemap references many nonexistent doc files
findings.evidence: afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:8-10 links guides/dev_setup.md, guides/validator.md, guides/factory.md; afi-docs/SUMMARY.md:13-18 links specs/architecture.md, specs/modules.md, specs/tokenomics.md, specs/governance.md - none of these exist in the tree
findings.recommendation: Prune or create the missing files; fix README Reference Index links too. Low risk but erodes navigability of the docs hub.

contradictions.doc_says: AFI_Repository_Map.md presents afi-pipeline (DAG execution engine) and afi-agents (agent implementations) as core repos with interdependency diagrams and a development workflow.
contradictions.code_does: No afi-pipeline, afi-agents (or afi-construct) repo exists in the workspace; the current census lists 28 afi-* repos + .github + afi-xerc20 and none of these names; the north star flags them as historical/stale.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-token metadata declares a Hardhat/Sepolia toolchain and an entrypoint src/AFICoordinator.sol.
contradictions.code_does: The repo is a Foundry/Base project (forge build) whose coordinator contract is src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol; there is no AFICoordinator.sol and no hardhat config.

contradictions.doc_says: Multiple docs-hub files each self-declare top authority (AGENTS.md wins; ARCHITECTURE_STATUS is the single source of truth; the Doctrine is authoritative).
contradictions.code_does: None of them names AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md - the actual north-star direction doc the hub hosts - as the canonical top of the hierarchy.

contradictions.doc_says: The Orchestrator Doctrine (copied into the docs hub, 'Status: Authoritative') states afi-reactor is THE orchestrator of AFI and 'the DAG is law'.
contradictions.code_does: The north star reframes reactor as the REFERENCE orchestrator and defines protocol law as conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions (any conforming orchestrator allowed).

contradictions.doc_says: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT presents the TSSD vault as MongoDB ('MongoDB TSSD vault persistence', 'afi-infra - TSSD vault (MongoDB)').
contradictions.code_does: The canonical vault.schema.json defines a multi-engine vault (mongodb/postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb), so MongoDB is one reference engine, not the vault itself.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-math codex metadata names its consumers as afi-engine and afi-token-finalized (among others).
contradictions.code_does: Those repos do not exist in the org; the actual downstream consumers are afi-token, afi-reactor, afi-core and afi-mint.
